reasoning: With only 24 days remaining in 2025, the base rate for a spontaneous Half-Life 3 announcement is extremely low, especially since multiple leakers claim it will not be at The Game Awards (Dec 11). While there is compelling datamined evidence of project 'HLX' and strong insider rumblings that Valve is aiming for a 2025 announcement alongside new hardware, Valve's notorious unpredictability ('Valve Time') and anti-leak 'canary traps' make any specific date highly suspect. Additionally, the primary source for the imminent December announcement (Mike Straw) has clarified he cannot confirm the project is actually Half-Life 3, and his single-source date could be inaccurate. Given that Tyler McVicker's earlier Q3/Summer and November announcement timelines proved incorrect, it is more likely that Valve's timeline will slip into 2026 (or that the announced game won't strictly qualify under the criteria). The 15% probability acknowledges the unprecedented recent volume of leaks and hardware momentum, while remaining appropriately skeptical of a pre-Jan 1st launch.
